content: The half-life of Carbon-14 is approximately 5,730 years. This means that after 5,730 years, half of the original amount of Carbon-14 in a sample will have decayed into nitrogen. This property makes Carbon-14 useful for radiocarbon dating archaeological artifacts and determining the age of organic materials.
raw_content: The half-life of Carbon-14 is approximately 5,730 years. This means that after 5,730 years, half of the original amount of Carbon-14 in a sample will have decayed into nitrogen.
